elements.text: C1-2-05, Delivering Green MBS to Fannie Mae (09/04/2024)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information about delivering Green MBS to Fannie Mae, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loan Delivery Data
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae's Green MBS consists of loans secured by properties that have received approved green building certifications within the last five years. An eligible green building certification requires the home to be at least 10% more efficient than a home built to an individual state's energy code. Lenders may deliver whole loans or MBS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Green MBS loans are subject to standard Selling Guide requirements, including the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans may be ARMs or fixed-rate.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: All property types are eligible.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Refer to the Eligibility Matrix for applicable LTV, CLTV and HCLTV ratio requirements.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The property must have one or more eligible green building certifications obtained within the last five years. See Single-Family Green MBS Certifications for a list of current organizations that meet our requirements.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For whole loans: The loan must be sold within five years of the property's certification year.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For loans in MBS: The pool must have a pool issuance year within five years of the property's certification year.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Example : For a loan secured by a property with a green building certification received in the year 2020, the loan must be sold to Fannie Mae by the end of 2025.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Delivery Data
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For a Green whole loan sale, lenders should contact their Fannie Mae Account Team (see  E-1-02, List of Contacts).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Green MBS, lenders may use EarlyCheck or Loan Delivery to confirm that all loans are Green MBS-eligible. To be considered a Green MBS, 100% of the loans in the MBS must be eligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: One of the following special feature codes must be delivered in addition to any other SFCs that may apply to the transaction:
elements.label: text

elements.text: SFC 784 - ENERGY STAR green building certification, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: SFC 881 - Department of Energy Zero Ready Home.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: C1-2-06, Bailee Letters (04/06/2022)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides information on bailee letters, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bailee Letters
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Resolution of Mismatched Delivery Instructions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Liability for Losses Incurred by a Mortgage Seller or any Other Party
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Bailee Letters
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Bailee letters typically state that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the notes identified in the letter are pledged to the named third party,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the lien of the third party's security interest will be released only if the proceeds from the transfer of the mortgages to Fannie Mae are delivered to the third party in accordance with the delivery instructions in the letter, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the recipient holds the notes as a 'bailee' (or in trust) for the third party until the third party either receives the proceeds (cash or securities) or receives back the notes for any mortgages that were not purchased or securitized.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The term 'bailee letter' includes any communication that notifies a document custodian of any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the notes are pledged to a third party;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the notes are subject to a security interest or lien held by a third party;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the notes are owned by, or titled in the name of, a third party (for example, mortgages that are subject to a sale and repurchase arrangement); or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the notes are subject to some other claim or interest held by a third party that would, if it were not released, result in Fannie Mae's acquiring less than clear, unencumbered, and exclusive title to the mortgage notes.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The requirements for processing bailee letters and other notification of third-party interests in mortgage loans can be found in Fannie Mae's RDC guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolution of Mismatched Delivery Instructions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will undertake to identify mismatches and to achieve matches in delivery instructions promptly, in good faith, and in accordance with reasonable business practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Liability for Losses Incurred by a Mortgage Seller or any Other Party
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will not be liable for any losses that a mortgage seller or any other party incurs as the result of Fannie Mae's actions or omissions related to these procedures, including Fannie Mae's execution of (or failure to execute) specific steps, and any resulting delay.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A mortgage seller is obligated to ensure that Fannie Mae takes free and clear title to the mortgages it delivers to Fannie Mae; therefore, it must make sure that its delivery instructions match those of its warehouse lender before it delivers the mortgage to Fannie Mae. Because of this obligation, the mortgage seller will be fully responsible for any trading or settlement delays (including those Fannie Mae experiences) that arise as the result of mismatches in delivery instructions. (See C3-7-06, Settling the Trade, for information on how to settle different delivery instructions.) Further, the execution of these procedures will not constitute a waiver of any of the rights and remedies Fannie Mae has under the mortgage seller's representation and warranty that the title to any mortgage note it delivers to Fannie Mae is free and clear of any security interest, lien, pledge, or other encumbrance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter C1-3, Loan Remittance Types Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When selling loans to Fannie Mae, lenders must decide if they will service the loans or release the servicing to another Fannie Mae-approved lender. They must also select a method for remitting borrower payments to Fannie Mae. This chapter provides an overview regarding remitting payments to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C1-3-01, General Information on Remittance Types (08/26/2014)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides an overview of borrower payment remittance types, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Actual/Actual
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Scheduled/Scheduled
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Scheduled/Actual
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When taking down a commitment to sell whole loans to Fannie Mae, lenders must select a remittance type that identifies both the amount and timing for sending the borrowers' mortgage payments to Fannie Mae. Not all remittance types are available for all product types. For MBS mortgages, the only remittance type available is Scheduled/Scheduled.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After Fannie Mae purchases a mortgage, it generally will not change the remittance type the lender has selected. Refer to the applicable section of the Servicing Guide for more information about remittance types.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Actual/Actual
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Actual/Actual (A/A) remittance type is available for whole loans. It is required for HUD-guaranteed and Rural Development-guaranteed mortgages. Lenders must notify Fannie Mae immediately and remit the actual principal and interest collected from the borrower, net of servicing fees, to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scheduled/Scheduled
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Scheduled/Scheduled (S/S) remittance type is available on a negotiated basis for whole loans, including ARMs, and fully amortizing fixed-rate conventional monthly payment first mortgages. It is required for MBS mortgages. Lenders remit scheduled interest (net of servicing fees) and scheduled principal due, whether or not payment is collected from the borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scheduled/Actual
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Scheduled/Actual (S/A) remittance type is available for whole loans. Lenders remit scheduled interest (net of servicing fees) regardless of actual collections from the borrower and actual principal collected the month following receipt of funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subpart C2, Whole Loan Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subpart includes Fannie Mae's policies, parameters, and other information regarding the sale and delivery of whole loans for cash. Whole loan products, commitment types and process, servicing fees, remittance types, loan payments and purchase adjustments are covered in the first chapter; requirements for good delivery, extensions and pair-offs, required data and documentation, funding and post-purchase adjustments in the second chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter C2-1, Mandatory and Best Efforts Commitments to Sell Whole Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: As noted in this chapter, when a lender enters into a mandatory commitment to sell whole loans to Fannie Mae, the lender agrees to deliver a specified dollar amount of loans, within certain tolerances, to Fannie Mae by a specified future date. Fannie Mae agrees to purchase those loans at an agreed-upon price. With a best efforts commitment, the lender agrees to deliver a specific loan to Fannie Mae by a specified date if that loan closes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The first section in this chapter provides the details associated with obtaining and fulfilling mandatory commitments to sell whole loans; the second section addresses best efforts commitments; and the third section describes the use of Servicing Marketplace in selling whole loans to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section C2-1.1, Mandatory Commitments to Sell Whole Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: C2-1.1-01, Mandatory Commitment Process (10/30/2009)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides information on the process lenders use to obtain a mandatory commitment to sell whole loans to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mandatory Commitment Process
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mandatory Commitment Process
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: To obtain a mandatory commitment to sell whole loans to Fannie Mae, lenders may use Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application, a free Web-based application available to subscribers, or may contact the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk (see E-1-02, List of Contacts). Lenders can obtain information on subscribing to the whole loan committing application by contacting their Fannie Mae customer account team or the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk, or by referring to Fannie Mae's website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders can obtain commitments on any weekday, with the exception of bond market observed holidays as defined by the Securities Industry and Financial Markets Association (SIFMA).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae confirms its acceptance of the lender's request for a mandatory commitment by electronically transmitting a commitment confirmation to the lender. If Fannie Mae is unable to process the electronic transmission, lenders are notified promptly. Should a lender have a problem receiving a commitment confirmation, it should immediately contact the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the lender has obtained the commitment by contacting the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk, the legally binding terms of the commitment are captured in a recorded telephone conversation between the lender and the Desk.
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: There are no recently issued Announcements related to this topic.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C2-1.1-02, General Information about Mandatory Commitment Pricing and Fees (06/07/2023)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic describes Fannie Mae's pricing policies and practices, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: As noted in  C1-1-01, Execution Options, when lenders commit to sell loans to Fannie Mae, Fannie Mae provides a 'live' price, so named because prices move throughout the day, generally in tandem with the MBS market. Fannie Mae purchases regularly amortizing whole mortgages either at par (100% of the unpaid principal balance), at a discount, or at a premium, based on the type of mortgage being delivered and the pricing option specified in the whole loan commitment under which the mortgage is delivered.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pass-Through Rates
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For whole loans, the rate at which the lender must remit interest on the mortgages it sells to Fannie Mae via a mandatory commitment is the pass-through rate, defined as the difference between the gross note rate and the servicing fee. Lenders can choose a range of five consecutive pass-through rates in increments of 0.125% under the same mandatory commitment. When obtaining a commitment, lenders specify the minimum pass-through rate. The lender does not need to know the exact interest rate of the mortgages that will be delivered in fulfillment of the commitment as long as the pass-through rates fall within the selected range. The actual interest payments lenders will remit for regularly amortizing mortgages will depend on the remittance option selected by the lender.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Access to Pricing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders obtain current pricing specific to their institution by accessing Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Access to both generic and lender-specific pricing information is available to approved Fannie Mae lenders and requires a valid user ID and password. For information on obtaining access to this information, lenders should contact their Fannie Mae customer account team or the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Up-front Commitment, Extension, Overdelivery, Pair-off and Other Fees
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Fannie Mae may charge up-front fees on commitments made to deliver adjustable-rate mortgages for which the lender has selected the scheduled/actual remittance option. See  C1-3-01, General Information on Remittance Types, for more information on this remittance type.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae may charge lenders for lender-requested and automatic extensions for mandatory commitments ('extensions'), delivering more than the maximum delivery amount of their commitment ('overdelivering'), repurchasing all or part of a commitment ('pairing-off'), or otherwise failing to satisfy mandatory commitment requirements. These fees, if charged, are drafted from the lender's designated account the business day following the assessment. If cash is due to the lender under a cash back pair-off, the lender's designated account will be credited the business day following the pair-off. See below for account requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Extension fees are based on three factors:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amount to be extended, which equals the lender's original commitment amount minus any deliveries as of the date of the extension, and, if the extension is a partial extension, minus any partial pair-off amount;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Actual number of days by which the commitment expiration date is being extended. (Note that if the request is for a 20-day extension and the 20th day falls on a weekend or holiday, the contract will be extended to the next business day.); and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Daily interest charge, which is based on the minimum pass-through rate posted on the original commitment.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For overdelivery and pair-off fees, including cash back pair-offs, whole loan prices captured at commitment and again at the time of the overdelivery or pair-off are used to determine if a fee will be charged or if cash back is due and, if so, the amount. All pair-off amounts (including cash back pair-offs) are calculated from the original commitment amount, not the low delivery tolerance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A lender must designate a non-MBS P&I custodial account from which Fannie Mae can and will automatically draft and credit whole loan transaction and related fees that it owes, or is owed by, Fannie Mae. The bank instructions for the account must be established in the  Cash Remittance System (CRS).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will draft or credit, as applicable, up-front commitment, extension, overdelivery, and pair-off fees from the lender's designated account on the day following the request for the commitment, extension, overdelivery, or pair-off. Compensatory fees for failure to satisfy mandatory delivery requirements will be drafted from the designated account on the first Friday after the expiration of the commitment period and any additional time allowed to effect the delivery or a pair-off.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To ensure that funds related to borrower payments are not used to satisfy a lender's corporate responsibility for the payment of fees or charges due Fannie Mae, a lender must make sure that its designated account will have sufficient funds on hand when Fannie Mae drafts it for the related fee. The lender must notify Fannie Mae immediately about changes to the status of its designated account to ensure Fannie Mae's drafts are honored and protect the lender from being charged additional fees for late remittances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Required Common Loan Attributes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: All loans delivered against a whole loan commitment must share certain common attributes. These include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Product type - See Mortgage Products for information about products eligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mortgage type - Conventional or government
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lien type - First mortgages
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Amortization type - Fixed-rate or adjustable-rate
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Mortgage term - 10-, 15-, 20-, or 30-year. Lenders may commit to deliver loans with nonstandard amortization terms or loans that exceed the maximum number of months for a given term by selecting the next highest amortization schedule available in Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application, regardless of pricing option.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: ARM plan number, if applicable
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Remittance type
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Commitment Amounts
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders may enter into multiple commitments but may not exceed $200 million in aggregate commitment volume per day. Lenders seeking permission to exceed this amount must contact the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk (see E-1-02, List of Contacts). Lenders exceeding the limit without Fannie Mae approval may be required to pair off their commitment(s) at the lender's expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: There is no minimum commitment amount for mandatory whole loan commitments. Note that commitments are issued in multiples of $1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Commitment Periods
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Commitments to deliver most loan products can be taken for 1 to 90 days. Lenders should be sure to choose a commitment period that allows sufficient time after loan closing for the fulfillment of the lender's shipping and delivery requirements. (See C2-2-01, General Requirements for Good Delivery of Whole Loans, and C2-2-02, Documentation Requirements for Whole Loan Deliveries, for details.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Commitments are based on calendar days but must expire on a business day. Lenders can track the status of their commitments in Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignments, Sales and Transfers of Whole Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: By submitting a whole loan to Fannie Mae as a whole loan delivery, the lender represents, warrants, and agrees that all right, title, and interest in the mortgage is sold, transferred, set over, and otherwise conveyed by the lender to Fannie Mae as of the date of Fannie Mae's funding of the purchase proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A lender may deliver a loan that has been originated by another lender. However, the selling lender must make all of the warranties specified in Fannie Mae's Contract as if it were the originator. In such cases, the selling lender must be aware of all matters related to the loan that were known to the originating lender.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender may not assign Fannie Mae's whole loan commitments except to obtain construction financing or interim financing under a 'warehouse' line of credit. If the construction or warehouse lender intends to use the commitment or contract to deliver mortgages to Fannie Mae on its own behalf, it must be an approved Fannie Mae lender. It also must notify the lender's Fannie Mae customer account team of the assignment before it delivers a loan to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Negotiated Commitments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders that want to sell loans to Fannie Mae that contain unique eligibility and underwriting considerations not permissible for delivery via a standard commitment may request a negotiated commitment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A variance between Fannie Mae and the lender is required when the lender is delivering whole loans via a negotiated commitment. Lenders should contact their Fannie Mae customer account team for details.

elements.text: C2-1.1-04, Mandatory Commitment Extensions and Pair-Offs (05/30/2017)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides details on lender-requested and automatically-generated extensions and pair-offs, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender-Requested Commitment Extensions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae-Implemented (Automatic) Commitment Extensions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender-Requested Pair-offs
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae-Implemented (Automatic) Pair-offs
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Additional Resources
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Under certain circumstances, lenders may extend or pair off a mandatory whole loan commitment. In addition, in certain circumstances, Fannie Mae will automatically extend commitment periods and pair off commitment balances. For information on the fees associated with these activities, see C2-1.1-02, General Information about Mandatory Commitment Pricing and Fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender-Requested Commitment Extensions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders may extend commitments on or before the original commitment expiration date for up to a maximum of 30 days from that date. They may request multiple extensions as long as the total extension period is no longer than 30 days. Each request must be made before the contract expiration date in effect following the previous extension. See C2-1.1-02, General Information about Mandatory Commitment Pricing and Fees, for information on the fees associated with a lender-requested extension.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders may extend the entire outstanding commitment balance or a portion of it, if the lender also requests a partial pair-off of the remaining balance. The sum of the extended amount and the paired-off amount must equal the total remaining commitment amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Lenders may request extensions in Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application or through the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk between 8:15 a.m. and 10:00 p.m. (Eastern time) on any business day (see E-1-02, List of Contacts). All telephone conversations are voice-recorded. Lenders should maintain detailed records of the contract extensions requested.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae-Implemented (Automatic) Commitment Extensions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If a lender fails to meet Fannie Mae's good delivery requirement within the original commitment period and has not previously extended the commitment, Fannie Mae will automatically extend it. Commitments expiring with outstanding unpaid principal balances will be automatically extended a minimum of five calendar days and assessed the appropriate extension fee. See C2-1.1-02, General Information about Mandatory Commitment Pricing and Fees, for information on the fees associated with an automatic extension.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If no deliveries have been made by the expiration of the five-day extension, Fannie Mae will effect an automatic pair-off and the appropriate fee assessed and drafted from the lender's designated account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender-Requested Pair-offs
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A lender may purchase (pair off) all or part of a mandatory delivery commitment in the whole loan committing application or by contacting the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk between 8:15 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. (Eastern time), if it is unable to satisfy Fannie Mae's minimum delivery requirement. The request can be made at any time between the date on which the commitment was executed and the date on which it expires. The lender can obtain a new quotation later, but before the commitment expiration date, to avoid an automatic extension and the possible assessment of an automatic pair-off fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After Fannie Mae verifies the information required for calculating a pair-off fee, it will provide the lender with a fee quotation through either the whole loan committing application or through the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk. The quotation may be for a pair-off fee to be paid by the lender or, if the transaction is eligible for a cash back pair-off, it may be for an amount due to the lender by Fannie Mae. The lender must accept the quotation within a short time frame if it wants to exercise the pair-off. Confirmation of the pair-off can be obtained in the whole loan committing application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In conjunction with a lender-requested pair-off, if the whole loan price at commitment is greater than the price at the time of the pair-off solely as a result of market fluctuations (and not from any other cause, including product-related pricing changes implemented by Fannie Mae), then Fannie Mae will not charge a lender a pairoff fee but will provide cash back to the lender (known as a cash back pair-off). The lender is not eligible for a cash back pair-off on a commitment if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the commitment gets paired off through the automatic pair-off process, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the mortgage products delivered under the commitment are deemed by Fannie Mae, in its sole discretion, to be ineligible per the Selling Guide.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Additionally, negotiated transactions with the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk and commitments that exceed the daily volume limit may not be eligible for cash back pair-offs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Though lenders are generally eligible to receive cash back on pair-offs upon approval to transact business in the whole loan committing application, Fannie Mae may decline to activate a lender for cash back pair-offs if, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Fannie Mae's sole discretion, the lender's anticipated business strategy does not align with Fannie Mae's use of the cash back pair-off process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, if any of the following events occur, Fannie Mae may immediately terminate a lender's eligibility for cash back pair-offs:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae, in its sole discretion, determines that the lender is obtaining commitments without the intent to deliver;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: there is any suspension of the lender's selling privileges under the Master Selling and Servicing Contract (MSSC); or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the lender's MSSC is terminated.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Termination will be effective as to any new or outstanding commitments as of the effective date of the termination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae-Implemented (Automatic) Pair-offs
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae automatically pairs off the entire remaining commitment balance for any expired, unfulfilled mandatory commitment that has been previously extended, although Fannie Mae will first process any mortgage deliveries pending in its purchase pipeline.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional Resources
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: See C2-1.1-02, General Information about Mandatory Commitment Pricing and Fees, for information on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the fees associated with a lender-requested pair-off,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: calculating cash back amounts, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the fees associated with an automatic pair-off.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: C2-1.1-05, Servicing Fees (05/01/2019)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic describes how Fannie Mae compensates lenders for servicing loans on its behalf, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determining and Obtaining a Servicing Fee
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Determining and Obtaining a Servicing Fee
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A lender's servicing compensation when obtaining mandatory commitments is described in its applicable servicing contract. Lenders must also specify a maximum allowable servicing fee, which may differ, depending on the mortgage product. For example, the maximum servicing fee for all fixed-rate loans is 50 basis points (.50%).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise provided in the related servicing contract, every month, lenders deduct their servicing fee from the borrower's interest payment before remitting the remainder to Fannie Mae. Lenders will receive a servicing fee from Fannie Mae only for the period during which it serviced the loan on Fannie Mae's behalf.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information regarding minimum and maximum servicing fees, refer to Servicing Guide F-2-09, Servicing Fees for Portfolio Mortgage Loans.

elements.text: C2-1.1-06, Accrued Interest Payments for Regularly Amortizing Mortgages (06/28/2011)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic describes the policies for accrued interest payments for regularly amortizing mortgages, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Accrued Interest Payments for Regularly Amortizing Mortgages
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The amount of servicing fee Fannie Mae will pay lenders for servicing regularly amortizing mortgages delivered via a mandatory commitment depends on the remittance option the lender has selected. (See C1-3-01, General Information on Remittance Types, for descriptions of the remittance types.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Accrued Interest Payments for Regularly Amortizing Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For A/A remittances, Fannie Mae purchases accrued interest from the last paid installment date for the mortgage up to, but not including, the purchase date. This interest adjustment is based on the unpaid principal balance of the mortgage at the time it is submitted for purchase and the designated pass-through rate of the mortgage (which is the lesser of the net note rate and Fannie Mae's required yield for mortgages delivered under whole loan commitments that specify the standard pricing option). If interest is prepaid, Fannie Mae deducts accrued interest from the purchase proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For S/S remittances, Fannie Mae purchases accrued interest from the first day of the purchase month up to, but not including, the purchase date. This interest adjustment is based on the scheduled unpaid principal balance for the mortgage as of the purchase date and the designated pass-through rate of the mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For S/A remittances, Fannie Mae purchases accrued interest from the first day of the purchase month up to, but not including, the purchase date. This interest adjustment is based on the unpaid principal balance for the mortgage at the time it is submitted for purchase and the designated pass-through rate of the mortgage (which is the lesser of the net note rate and Fannie Mae's required yield for mortgages delivered under cash commitments that specify the standard pricing option).
elements.label: text

elements.text: C2-1.1-07, Standard ARM and Converted ARM Resale Commitments (05/30/2017)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides information about whole loan mandatory commitments to deliver ARM loans to Fannie Mae, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standard ARM Mandatory Commitments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Minimum Commitment Amount for Converted ARM Resale Commitments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Process for Converting an ARM within an MBS to a Fixed-Rate Mortgage
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Commitment Timing, Pricing and Compensatory Fees for Converted ARM Resale Commitments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Standard ARM Mandatory Commitments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders may take down mandatory whole loan commitments to deliver a variety of ARMs to Fannie Mae. Fannie Mae will purchase ARMs with varying initial fixed rate periods and indexes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See the Standard ARM Plan Matrix for parameters for ARM loans eligible for sale to Fannie Mae, including the initial fixed interest rate period, interest rate adjustment frequency, indexes, and cap structure. Fannie Mae's standard ARM program does not permit the purchase of ARMs with caps ('floors') (other than the ARM's margin) on the lifetime decreases to the life of the loan. The margin for each ARM delivered must include the same servicing fee specified in the commitment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: For additional information, see the Standard ARM Plan Matrix.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Commitment Amount for Converted ARM Resale Commitments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Converted ARM Resale mandatory commitments are used by lenders to redeliver ARMs that were originally in an MBS pool and have been converted to fixed-rate whole loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders must agree to deliver an amount (total UPB) equal to the outstanding balance of the mortgage to be delivered under the commitment. If the lender is delivering multiple mortgages with the same interest rate after conversion, the amount must equal the combined UPB of those mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Process for Converting an ARM within an MBS to a Fixed-Rate Mortgage
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: ARM loans with a conversion option are eligible for conversion to fixed-rate mortgages. When a borrower chooses to convert an ARM currently in an MBS pool, the lender must follow these steps.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Redeliver the mortgage to Fannie Mae as a whole loan (A/A remittance only), if the lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified a take-out post-conversion disposition option when it originally delivered the ARM.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Continue any recourse or credit enhancement that initially applied to the mortgage unless the
elements.label: text

elements.text: lender and Fannie Mae agree otherwise.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Obtain a Converted ARM Resale commitment number from Fannie Mae.

elements.text: Section C2-1.2, Best Efforts Commitments to Sell Whole Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: C2-1.2-01, Best Efforts Commitment Process (08/06/2025)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic describes basic information about obtaining a best efforts commitment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Best Efforts Commitment Process
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Best Efforts Commitment Process
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: To obtain a best efforts commitment, lenders may use Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application, a free Web-based application available to subscribers, or may contact the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk. Lenders can obtain information on subscribing to the whole loan committing application by contacting their Fannie Mae customer account team or the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk, or by referring to Fannie Mae's website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Fannie Mae confirms its acceptance of the lender's request for a best efforts delivery commitment by providing a confirmation to the lender that the loan has been committed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional information may also be obtained by reviewing Pricing & Execution on Fannie Mae's website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C2-1.2-02, Best Efforts Commitment Pricing, Periods, and Fees (03/03/2021)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides information on best efforts commitments, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Best Efforts Commitment Pricing
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Best Efforts Pair-off Fees and Duplicate Commitment Price Adjustments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Best Efforts Commitment Periods and Extension Fees
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Best Efforts Commitment Pricing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: As is the case with mandatory whole loan commitments, Fannie Mae offers lenders a 'live' price in Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application as its purchase price for loans delivered via best efforts commitments. Live prices move throughout the day, generally in tandem with the MBS market. Fannie Mae may charge fees or make pricing adjustments in conjunction with a best efforts commitment. See also C1-1-01, Execution Options.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Best Efforts Pair-off Fees and Duplicate Commitment Price Adjustments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Under the best efforts commitment option, generally a pair-off fee will not be assessed for commitments that fallout due to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: cancellation of an applicant's rate lock, as long as the cancellation results from an applicant withdrawal or lender declination; or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: a change to the product type or key data which results in a change of the committed loan from a loan eligible for a best efforts commitment to a loan ineligible for a best efforts commitment.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: A pair-off fee will be assessed for commitments where the fallout event is due to a failure, for any reason, to deliver the closed and committed loan to Fannie Mae, including when a lender (or a third-party originator who originates in the lender's name) has solicited new pricing and committed or sold the loan to Fannie Mae or another investor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Fannie Mae may cancel a commitment and charge a pair-off fee in the event the property address is missing or fictitious or in the event the identical property address is used to enter into multiple commitments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A duplicate commitment price adjustment will be assessed in the event a lender delivers a loan to Fannie Mae against any other (duplicate) commitment(s) for the same borrower and property address committed prior to or within 30 days of the original commitment's fallout or expiration date (the earlier of the two).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Best Efforts Commitment Periods and Extension Fees
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: As is the case with mandatory commitments, best efforts commitment periods may be as short as one day and as long as 90 days. Lenders may extend commitments on or before the original commitment expiration date for up to a maximum of 30 days for a fee. Lenders may request multiple extensions as long as the total extension period is not longer than 30 days. A best efforts commitment with a 'Closed' loan status will automatically extend for five calendar days until the loan is purchased or the commitment has been extended for a maximum of 60 calendar days from the original expiration date. After a commitment has reached its maximum allowed auto-extension period, the whole loan committing application will automatically pair off the 'Closed' status commitment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae will notify lenders via the whole loan committing application of fees associated with extending the commitment period. Once notice is given, payment of the fee becomes an absolute obligation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : All committing fees and post purchase price adjustments will be drafted from the lender's draft account with Fannie Mae. All draft fees can be viewed on Fannie Mae's website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C2-1.2-03, Best Efforts Commitment Terms, Amounts, and Other Requirements (03/03/2021)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides information on terms and other requirements associated with best efforts commitments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: including:
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Best Efforts Commitment Terms and Amounts
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Best Efforts Servicing Options
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Other Best Efforts Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: DU Recommendations on Loans Delivered with Best Efforts Commitments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Best Efforts Commitment Terms and Amounts
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: By accepting any prices and delivering mortgages to Fannie Mae pursuant to a commitment obtained through Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application, the lender confirms that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lender is approved and continues to satisfy the eligibility requirements as described in this Guide, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the mortgages comply with all Fannie Mae requirements in the Mortgage Selling and Servicing Contract and the Selling and Servicing Guides .
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae will commit to pay to lenders proceeds that are based upon the acquisition price for committed loans, subject to any applicable adjustments, as long as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the key data and product type delivered matches the key data and product type reported to Fannie Mae to enter into the commitment, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the acquisition date is on or before the date upon which the commitment expires.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Best efforts commitments are for a specific borrower, address, and commitment period. Loan substitution is not allowed. Lenders may not change a best efforts commitment to a mandatory commitment (or vice versa) or change the remittance type from scheduled/scheduled to actual/actual (or vice versa).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lenders must notify Fannie Mae via the whole loan committing application within one business day of any change to the key data or product type that occurs during the commitment period. If there is a change to the key data or product type and the new loan meets Fannie Mae's eligibility requirements, the acquisition price for the committed loan may be changed to reflect the revised information (using worse case pricing when applicable).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Best Efforts Servicing Options
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders may retain, release, or sell the servicing rights associated with the loans they deliver in fulfillment of a best efforts commitment. Lenders may also execute a concurrent transfer of servicing via the whole loan committing application. If approved to participate in the Servicing Marketplace component of the whole loan committing application, lenders have the option to retain servicing or arrange for the concurrent sale of servicing to a participating servicer through the Servicing Marketplace. See C2-1.3-01, Servicing Marketplace, for more information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Those retaining or releasing servicing may do so using the actual/actual or scheduled/scheduled remittance type. Certain products, however, require servicing via a specific remittance type. See C1-3-01, General Information on Remittance Types, for information on remittance types and the Cash Remittance System for details on remittance requirements for specific mortgage products.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: For more information and to learn about additional terms and conditions associated with servicing-released executions, lenders should contact their Fannie Mae customer account team or the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk for details.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Other Best Efforts Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae's active document custodians serve as custodians for loans delivered via a best efforts execution. For additional information on the role of document custodians, see C2-2-02, Documentation Requirements for Whole Loan Deliveries. Also, refer to Fannie Mae's Active Document Custodians for a complete list of eligible custodians.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Early funding is available for loans delivered in fulfillment of a best efforts commitment. For details on this option and other options, and terms and conditions associated with best efforts commitments, lenders should contact their Fannie Mae customer account team or the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: DU Recommendations on Loans Delivered with Best Efforts Commitments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A lender may fulfill a best efforts commitment by delivering a mortgage loan with a valid DU loan casefile ID that is associated with the lender's institution ID. The DU loan casefile must have received a DU recommendation of Approve/Eligible no earlier than 60 days prior to commitment. Alternatively, lenders may select the 'Other' Underwriting Method (as viewed in the whole loan committing application), in which case a DU recommendation is not required.

elements.text: C2-1.3-01, Servicing Marketplace (03/03/2021)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic describes the process for concurrent sale of servicing using the Servicing Marketplace component of Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Concurrent Sale of Servicing
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Relationship Between Lenders and Servicers
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: No Recourse to Fannie Mae
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Number of Servicers in Servicing Marketplace
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Concurrent Sale of Servicing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders may retain, release, or sell the servicing rights associated with the loans they deliver to Fannie Mae. Lenders may execute a concurrent transfer of servicing via Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application with either a best efforts or a mandatory commitment. By participating in the servicing-released component of the whole loan committing application, known as Servicing Marketplace, lenders may arrange for a concurrent sale of the servicing to an approved Fannie Mae servicer. See A3-3-02, Concurrent Servicing Transfers, for approval requirements and additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All transfers of servicing using Servicing Marketplace must comply with this Guide and all applicable laws. The procedures and documentation required for a transfer of servicing are set out in the servicing transfer instructions available directly from the associated Servicing Marketplace transferee servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A lender's rights and obligations related to a committed loan for which the lender has sold the servicing are as set forth in the Mortgage Selling and Servicing Contract and the Selling and Servicing Guides , and use of the whole loan committing application in no way alters a lender's obligations to Fannie Mae with respect to such loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Relationship Between Lenders and Servicers
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders and servicers using Servicing Marketplace must execute a purchase and sale agreement that governs the sale of servicing rights between lenders and servicers as provided herein, or lenders and servicers will be subject to the terms and conditions of the mortgage loan purchase and sale agreement set forth in E-2-05, Servicing Marketplace - Mortgage Loan Servicing Purchase and Sale Agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: No Recourse to Fannie Mae
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Servicers are encouraged to use the Servicing Marketplace rate sheet to establish the price they will pay a seller for servicing. Fannie Mae's calculation of the net SRP and the net funding SRP using the servicer's rate sheet is based on unpaid principal balance, escrow funds, and other loan information submitted by the lender at loan delivery. Fannie Mae accepts no responsibility for the accuracy or completeness of such information and is entitled to rely on such information in calculating the net SRP and, if the servicer uses the 'All-In Funding'
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: feature of Servicing Marketplace, in collecting an amount equal to the net funding SRP from the servicer and remitting it to the lender on behalf of the servicer (or in deducting an amount equal to any negative net funding SRP from the acquisition proceeds and remitting it to the active servicer). Lenders will have no recourse to Fannie Mae if issues arise with any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the accuracy or completeness of the information used to calculate the net SRP or the net funding SRP;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the accuracy and completeness of any information that the lender obtains from the whole loan committing application and uses or relies upon in any way;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the lender's sale (or attempted sale) of servicing to the servicer (other than the calculation, collection, and remittance of the net funding SRP and transfer of certain data to the servicer); or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the servicing obligations and obligations relating to escrow funds.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Number of Servicers in Servicing Marketplace
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae may increase or decrease the number of servicers that participate in Servicing Marketplace at any time.

elements.text: Chapter C2-2, Whole Loan Deliveries to and Purchasing by Fannie Mae
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Whether selling whole loans to Fannie Mae under the terms of a mandatory, best efforts or converted ARM resale commitment, lenders receive the sale proceeds when they have made 'good delivery.' This means that the mortgages delivered meet all of Fannie Mae's underwriting and legal criteria and satisfy the terms of the commitment under which they are being delivered. This chapter provides information on the lender's requirements for making good delivery and the policies and procedures Fannie Mae uses to fund Fannie Mae's purchases. See C1-2-02, Loan Data and Documentation Delivery Requirements, for general information about this subject.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: C2-2-01, General Requirements for Good Delivery of Whole Loans (08/07/2018)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic provides Fannie Mae's requirements for good delivery of whole loans, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Definition of Whole Loan Good Delivery
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Amortization Schedule Requirements for Whole Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Limitations on High-Balance Whole Loan Deliveries
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Whole Loan Good Delivery Amounts
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overdeliveries of Whole Loan Commitments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Extending and Pairing-Out of Whole Loan Commitments
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Definition of Whole Loan Good Delivery
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: To receive funding for loans delivered in fulfillment of a mandatory, best efforts, or converted ARM resale commitment, lenders must make 'good delivery,' meaning the loans delivered must meet all of Fannie Mae's underwriting and legal criteria and satisfy the terms of the original commitment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae's good delivery requirements are not met unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable error-free mortgage documents and data reach the Fannie Mae-approved document custodian no later than the fi rst morning delivery , the day prior to the expiration date of the commitment;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the data reflected on the Loan Schedules pass all of the Fannie Mae purchasing edits;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the aggregate unpaid principal balance of all mortgages delivered under the commitment at least equals Fannie Mae's minimum required delivery amount (but does not exceed the maximum delivery amount on the expiration day of the commitment); and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the aggregate unpaid principal balance of all mortgages delivered under the commitment does not exceed Fannie Mae's delivery limits for loans with nonstandard characteristics, such as high-balance loans.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: To make good delivery on a best efforts commitment, the information concerning the loan that is delivered must match the key data elements specified in Fannie Mae's whole loan committing application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amortization Schedule Requirements for Whole Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For whole loans delivered to Fannie Mae, the first payment date must be no later than two months from the final disbursement date of the loan proceeds. In the case of a single-close construction-to-permanent loan, the two month period begins at the time of the conversion to permanent financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table provides an example of this requirement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: If the final disbursement date occurs in...
elements.label: text

elements.text: Then the first payment date must be no later than...
elements.label: text

elements.text: January
elements.label: text

elements.text: March
elements.label: text

elements.text: February
elements.label: text

elements.text: April
elements.label: text

elements.text: November
elements.label: text

elements.text: January
elements.label: text

elements.text: Limitations on High-Balance Whole Loan Deliveries
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following outlines key whole loan requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the whole loan committing application, high-balance 10-, 15-, 20-, and 30-year fully amortizing fixedrate mortgage loans are eligible for delivery under standard whole loan mandatory commitments, as long as the high-balance loans do not comprise more than 10% of the aggregate unpaid principal balance of the commitment. If the delivery of a high-balance mortgage loan causes the lender to exceed the 10% limitation, the lender is required to deliver the mortgage loan against a high-balance whole loan commitment. High-balance products are available in the whole loan committing application for this purpose.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note : 10-year fully amortizing fixed-rate high-balance mortgage loans are delivered under a 15-year high-balance commitment and 20-year fully amortizing fixed-rate high-balance mortgage loans are delivered under a 30-year high-balance commitment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The 10% limitation does not apply in the whole loan committing application to high-balance mortgage loans originated with non-TBA-eligible products (for example, ARMs) which may be delivered against standard conforming whole loan commitments, with no restriction on concentration.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: For best efforts commitments in the whole loan committing application, 15-year and 30-year fully amortizing fixed-rate high-balance mortgage loans must be delivered under 15-year or 30-year highbalance commitments, respectively. High-balance mortgage loans with 10- or 20-year fixed-rate terms or non-TBA-eligible products (such as ARMs) are not accommodated for best efforts commitments.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: See B5-1-01, High-Balance Mortgage Loan Eligibility and Underwriting and B5-1-02, High-Balance Pricing, Mortgage Insurance, Special Feature Codes, and Delivery Limitations for additional information about highbalance mortgage loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whole Loan Good Delivery Amounts
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: To make good delivery on a mandatory commitment, lenders must deliver loans for which the total UPB does not fall below the greater of $10,000 or 2.5% of the original commitment amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum required delivery amount is an amount that will not fall below the original commitment amount by more than the greater of $10,000 or 2.5% of the original commitment amount, unless a lender requests a partial pair-off of a commitment. If that occurs, the minimum required delivery amount will be reduced to $50 below the revised commitment amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The maximum delivery amount is an amount that will not exceed the original commitment amount by more than the greater of $10,000 or 2.5% of the original commitment amount, unless a lender requests an overdelivery. If a lender requests an overdelivery, the maximum required delivery amount will be $50 above the revised commitment amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overdeliveries of Whole Loan Commitments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders may request permission via the whole loan committing application for mandatory commitments or by contacting the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk (see E-1-02, List of Contacts) during regular business hours to over deliver, or deliver a total UPB that exceeds the maximum delivery amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The maximum overdelivery amount is 25% of the original commitment amount. For example, on an original commitment amount of $150,000, the maximum overdelivery amount is an additional $37,500 (25% of $150,000). In this example, the total delivered against the commitment cannot exceed $187,550 ($150,000 + $37,500 + $50).
elements.label: text

elements.text: After an overdelivery occurs, the maximum delivery tolerance level decreases to $50 above the new commitment amount. For commitments for which the overdelivery calculation (based on 25% of the original commitment amount) is less than $10,000, the high tolerance level becomes the maximum amount the lender can deliver.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae generally bases its decision on whether an overdelivery price adjustment will be due based on the movement in market prices between the date the commitment was originally obtained and the date on which Fannie Mae approved the overdelivery. If a price adjustment is due, Fannie Mae will draft an overdelivery price adjustment from the lender's designated account on the following business day.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Extending and Pairing-Out of Whole Loan Commitments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For information about extending and pairing-out of whole loan commitments, see C2-1.1-04, Mandatory Commitment Extensions and Pair-Offs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Recent Related Announcements
elements.label: text

elements.text: There are no recently issued Announcements related to this topic.

elements.text: C2-2-02, Documentation Requirements for Whole Loan Deliveries (05/01/2019)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic describes the documentation requirements for whole loan deliveries, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Required Documents for Whole Loans
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Submission of Documents for Whole Loans to a Document Custodian
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Whole Loan Deliveries to a Document Custodian
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Required Documents for Whole Loans
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: See E-2-01, Required Custodial Documents, for a list of the mortgage documents that must be included in the document submission package for all loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Submission of Documents for Whole Loans to a Document Custodian
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgage document submission packages must be submitted to the document custodian via overnight delivery (with instructions for first morning delivery) for the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: whole loans,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: As Soon As Pooled Plus (ASAP Plus), and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: As Soon As Pooled (ASAP Sale).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: All packages should be sent to the assigned document custodian and addressed according to the instructions provided by that custodian. The document custodian will certify the loans in accordance with the Master Custodial Agreement (Form 2017) in effect for Fannie Mae, the lender, and itself.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whole Loan Deliveries to a Document Custodian
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders are required to submit data for whole loan deliveries to Fannie Mae via Fannie Mae's Loan Delivery application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To ensure timely delivery to the document custodian, the seller should ensure the loan document submission package is submitted to its overnight carrier (with instructions for first morning delivery) the same day that the seller submits the loan data to Fannie Mae for purchase. Loan data delivered by the seller to Fannie Mae will be certified the next business day provided the documents have been delivered to the document custodian via first morning delivery, and there are no discrepancies and no unresolved lien issues.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the document custodian receives the mortgage document submission package, it will review the related loan documentation to verify that all of the required documents have been received and are in order. The document custodian will then work with the lender to correct any certification data or document errors. Lenders can opt-in to receive email notices through Loan Delivery when data or document discrepancies are noted during certification. Lenders then have the option to accept or decline data changes the custodian proposes as a result of the discrepancy on the note and the data they provided. The lender must work with the custodian to ensure all discrepancies are resolved.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there are no document or data-related errors, the document custodian will transmit a certification to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C2-2-03, General Information on Whole Loan Purchasing Policies (05/04/2022)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic includes information on the conditions under which Fannie Mae will fund the purchase of a loan delivered in fulfillment of a whole loan commitment, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whole Loan Funding Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Warehouse Lender's Release of Interest in the Property
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Procedures Related to the Secure Transfer of Funds
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Whole Loan Funding Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will send the proceeds from the whole loan sale via a wire transfer into the lender's designated account on the "Purchased and Funded" date (as reflected in Loan Delivery) if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the designated payee code is valid;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the loan is eligible for purchase; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the document custodian received a complete, accurate mortgage document submission package and loan delivery data by the applicable cutoff times.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Fannie Mae will direct the wire transfer to either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the account(s) and depository institution(s) the lender designates, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the lender itself (if it is able to receive wire transfers through the Fedwire system).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: To avoid delays in purchasing and funding, Fannie Mae accepts delivery of any mortgage up to 45 days from the due date of the reported last paid installment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Warehouse Lender's Release of Interest in the Property
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: When Fannie Mae purchases a mortgage, the mortgage seller represents and warrants that title to the mortgage note is free and clear of any security interest, lien, pledge, or other encumbrance, which means that any interest held by a warehouse lender must be released no later than the date Fannie Mae acquires the note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To ensure that this is always the case, Fannie Mae has established delivery procedures to cover those instances in which the document custodian either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: receives with the mortgage delivery documents a bailee letter notifying it of a warehouse lender's security interest, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: is otherwise aware that a warehouse lender is claiming an interest of any kind in the mortgage notes being delivered.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: This process is intended solely to ensure the correctness of the selling representations and warranties a mortgage seller makes to Fannie Mae (and not to benefit any third party). These procedures are outlined in C1-2-06, Bailee Letters. Also see C1-2-03, Ownership of Mortgage Loans Prior to Purchase or Securitization and Third-Party Security Interests, for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae also has established operational procedures that can be used to reduce instances of conflicting delivery instructions for pledged mortgages. See C1-1-01, Execution Options.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Procedures Related to the Secure Transfer of Funds
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Each lender is responsible for establishing and maintaining controls and procedures that ensure the confidentiality of all transfer instructions and payee codes and the integrity of its communications with Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender agrees to be bound by any transfer instructions issued in its name and sent to Fannie Mae, whether or not they were authorized.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A lender is solely liable for transfers that are initiated (either directly or indirectly) as the result of a breach in its security arrangements or as the result of its failure to give Fannie Mae timely notice of an error, omission, or irregularity in establishing payee arrangements. The lender is fully responsible for notifying the Fannie Mae Asset Acquisitions by telephone and submitting written confirmation of the call to Fannie Mae within 24 hours of the breach. Fannie Mae will do everything possible to suspend operations until a correction is sent, including removing all payee codes on record for the lender and issuing new codes based on instructions the lender provides in accordance with Fannie Mae standard procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender is advised that these procedures will not be used to detect an error in the transmission or content of a transfer of funds to the lender's designated bank or an error in processing a request to set up a payee code for use in transferring funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C2-2-04, Timing of Distribution of Whole Loan Purchase Proceeds (05/04/2022)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic describes the timing of Fannie Mae's funding of loans it purchases, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Timing of Distribution of Proceeds for Regularly Amortizing Mortgages
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Impact of Late Submissions of Required Data and Documentation
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Early Funding Options
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Timing of Distribution of Proceeds for Regularly Amortizing Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will fund the purchase proceeds for whole loan deliveries of regularly amortizing mortgages provided the following procedure has already occurred:
elements.label: text

elements.text: lender has electronically submitted clean, error-free loan delivery data through Loan Delivery by 9:00 p.m. (Eastern time),
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the document custodian has received a complete and accurate mortgage document submission package from a lender by first morning delivery the following day, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the document custodian has certified the mortgage document submission package.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Following the funding of the purchase proceeds the loan status in Loan Delivery will be "Purchased and Funded."
elements.label: text

elements.text: Impact of Late Submissions of Required Data and Documentation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If the required delivery data and documentation are not received by the applicable cutoff time, Fannie Mae will disburse the purchase proceeds on the business day following the date it receives these materials, assuming they are in order and the mortgage is eligible for purchase.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Early Funding Options
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae offers approved lenders the ability to receive funding at earlier points in the process. For information on Fannie Mae's Early Funding Options and Loan Delivery, the application lenders use to submit requests for early funding, see Fannie Mae's website or contact the Capital Markets Pricing and Sales Desk (see E-1-02, List of Contacts).
